entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop grown in India, susceptible to downy mildew.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A widespread and economically important disease of pearl millet causing substantial yield losses.

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where pearl millet is cultivated and downy mildew is prevalent.

entities.name: DM RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to resist infection by downy mildew.

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL PEDIGREE BREEDING
entities.description: A traditional breeding approach used to develop disease-resistant hybrids.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BACKCROSS BREEDING
entities.description: A modern breeding technique using genetic markers to transfer resistance genes.

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS F1 HYBRIDS
entities.description: Specific hybrid genotypes of pearl millet often susceptible to downy mildew.

entities.name: VIRULENT PATHOTYPES
entities.description: Strains of Sclerospora graminicola that have evolved to overcome hybrid resistance.

entities.name: YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: The reduction in crop productivity caused by downy mildew infection.
